comment.body:
Listen guys,
if you have read this post:
https://x.com/alma18499/status/1967932847022375351

the biggest takeaway from here is to keep in mind that the house always wins on the long-run.
No matter your hit-rate, no matter, your accuracy. Even if you would have a hit rate of 100%, meaning, you get every move accurately on every day of a year, you will lose, bcs the odds are designed to produce a negative EV for you on the long-run! (not only on the options market)

This is why I keep saying:
trade little, patiently, and wait for setups that actually clear and stuff.
You are not forced/obligated to spend your days at the desk.
For a wealthier person, daytrading is only a side thing, and doesn't uses more than 10% of his total wealth on daytrading (and not using the same account for long-term trades/investments)

Okay? :))

Educational post is out soon, I was sick in the past days, high fever, terrible headache + occupations like teaching and studying etc.
I will go in details...
write your wishes btw
